scope: Influential PAC papers listed in influential-pac-paper-ids.txt

assignment_rules.content_tags: Assign only topics that are central to the paper's problem, main results, or principal applications. Tags are faceted so a paper may have field, learning-model, and technical-subject tags.
assignment_rules.proof_tags: Assign salient argument techniques evidenced by substantive proofs in the extracted theorem JSON. These are paper-level aggregates, not replacements for each theorem's proofOrganization field. Generic direct algebra and the location of a proof are intentionally not tags.

content_tags.name: Agnostic learning
content_tags.description: Learning relative to the best available hypothesis without assuming the target belongs to the hypothesis class.

content_tags.name: Attribute-efficient learning
content_tags.description: Learning guarantees whose dependence on irrelevant or ambient attributes is especially small.

content_tags.name: Automata learning
content_tags.description: Learning finite automata or automaton-representable languages.

content_tags.name: Bayesian inference
content_tags.description: Learning with prior distributions, posterior-like weighting, or Bayesian model combination.

content_tags.name: Boolean function learning
content_tags.description: Learning Boolean concepts such as CNF, DNF, thresholds, formulas, or related representation classes.

content_tags.name: Boosting
content_tags.description: Combining weak hypotheses to obtain a high-accuracy predictor.

content_tags.name: Classification noise
content_tags.description: Random corruption of example labels, typically below noise rate one half.

content_tags.name: Combinatorial optimization
content_tags.description: Learning results formulated through or reduced to discrete optimization problems such as set cover.

content_tags.name: Complexity theory
content_tags.description: Classical computational-complexity assumptions, completeness, or reductions central to the paper.

content_tags.name: Computational hardness
content_tags.description: Intractability or non-learnability under computational assumptions.

content_tags.name: Concept learning
content_tags.description: Learning a target concept from labeled examples in a formal concept class.

content_tags.name: Cryptographic hardness
content_tags.description: Hardness of learning obtained from the assumed security of cryptographic primitives.

content_tags.name: Cryptography
content_tags.description: Cryptographic primitives or security problems are a central part of the paper.

content_tags.name: Decision lists
content_tags.description: Representation and learnability of ordered if-then decision rules.

content_tags.name: Decision-theoretic learning
content_tags.description: Learning formulated as choosing actions or predictors to minimize a general loss.

content_tags.name: Generalization bounds
content_tags.description: Bounds transferring empirical or training performance to population or test performance.

content_tags.name: Hidden-variable learning
content_tags.description: Learning problems in which relevant explanatory variables are unobserved.

content_tags.name: Inductive bias
content_tags.description: Quantifying how restrictions or preferences over hypotheses affect learnability.

content_tags.name: Information theory
content_tags.description: Information measures such as relative entropy are central to the learning bounds.

content_tags.name: Kernel methods
content_tags.description: Generalization or complexity analysis for kernel predictors and kernel expansions.

content_tags.name: Lower bounds
content_tags.description: Necessary sample, query, representation, or computational resources for learning.

content_tags.name: Malicious-noise learning
content_tags.description: Learning when an adaptive or worst-case adversary corrupts examples.

content_tags.name: Mistake bounds
content_tags.description: Worst-case bounds on prediction mistakes in sequential learning.

content_tags.name: Model averaging
content_tags.description: Prediction by a weighted mixture rather than selection of one model.

content_tags.name: Multiplicative weights
content_tags.description: Learners or decision rules based on multiplicative updates of hypothesis, expert, or feature weights.

content_tags.name: Neural networks
content_tags.description: Capacity, generalization, or learnability of neural-network architectures.

content_tags.name: Noise-tolerant learning
content_tags.description: Learning algorithms and guarantees that remain valid under corrupted examples.

content_tags.name: Occam bounds
content_tags.description: Generalization from compact or nearly minimal hypotheses consistent with the data.

content_tags.name: Online learning
content_tags.description: Sequential prediction and updating against a stream of outcomes.

content_tags.name: PAC learning
content_tags.description: Distribution-free probably approximately correct learning from random examples.

content_tags.name: PAC-Bayes
content_tags.description: Distribution-free PAC guarantees for predictors or mixtures controlled by a prior.

content_tags.name: Parity learning
content_tags.description: Learning parity concepts or using parity classes to establish limitations.

content_tags.name: Probability theory
content_tags.description: Probability-theoretic convergence or deviation results are the paper's primary subject.

content_tags.name: Pseudo-dimension
content_tags.description: Real-valued analogue of VC dimension used to control function classes.

content_tags.name: Query complexity
content_tags.description: Number or precision of oracle queries required for learning.

content_tags.name: Rademacher and Gaussian complexity
content_tags.description: Data-dependent function-class complexity measured with random signs or Gaussian variables.

content_tags.name: Real-valued function learning
content_tags.description: Learning real-valued predictors, losses, regressors, or function classes.

content_tags.name: Robust learning
content_tags.description: Learning guarantees designed to withstand contamination or adversarially bad data.

content_tags.name: Sample complexity
content_tags.description: Sufficient or necessary number of examples for a learning guarantee.

content_tags.name: Statistical query learning
content_tags.description: Learning through approximate expectations rather than access to individual labeled examples.

content_tags.name: Uniform convergence
content_tags.description: Uniform control of empirical frequencies, risks, or losses over a class.

content_tags.name: VC theory
content_tags.description: Growth functions, shattering, and Vapnik-Chervonenkis dimension.

content_tags.name: Weak learning
content_tags.description: Learning with only a guaranteed advantage over chance or a weak accuracy target.

proof_tags.name: Adversarial construction
proof_tags.description: Builds a hard distribution, target, sample, sequence, or corruption strategy against an arbitrary learner.

proof_tags.name: Algorithmic construction
proof_tags.description: Proves the result by explicitly constructing and analyzing an algorithm, hypothesis, representation, or witness.

proof_tags.name: Combinatorial counting
proof_tags.description: Uses cardinality, growth functions, shattering patterns, encodings, binomial estimates, or related counting arguments.

proof_tags.name: Comparison and contraction inequalities
proof_tags.description: Compares stochastic complexities or contracts a composed class through Lipschitz or Gaussian comparison arguments.

proof_tags.name: Concentration and union bounds
proof_tags.description: Turns pointwise random guarantees into high-probability uniform statements using tail inequalities or union bounds.

proof_tags.name: Constrained optimization
proof_tags.description: Solves a constrained extremum, commonly with convexity, duality, or Lagrange multipliers.

proof_tags.name: Contradiction
proof_tags.description: Assumes the negation or a forbidden learner exists and derives an explicit contradiction.

proof_tags.name: Convexity and analytic inequalities
proof_tags.description: Relies materially on convexity, calculus, logarithmic or exponential estimates, or comparable analytic inequalities.

proof_tags.name: Game-theoretic argument
proof_tags.description: Reasons through competing strategies, allocation games, or minimax-style adversarial interaction.

proof_tags.name: Geometric argument
proof_tags.description: Uses hyperplanes, orthants, metric covers or packings, or geometric separation.

proof_tags.name: Induction and recursion
proof_tags.description: Uses induction over structure or rounds, or a recursive construction with maintained invariants.

proof_tags.name: Information-theoretic inequality
proof_tags.description: Uses relative entropy, KL divergence, change of measure, or another information inequality.

proof_tags.name: Potential-function argument
proof_tags.description: Tracks a scalar potential, often a total weight or exponential loss, whose evolution proves progress.

proof_tags.name: Probabilistic analysis
proof_tags.description: Uses random choice, averaging, expectations, or event probabilities as a substantive part of the argument.

proof_tags.name: Quantifier reversal
proof_tags.description: Changes the order of high-probability and randomized-concept quantifiers through an explicit measure argument.

proof_tags.name: Reduction
proof_tags.description: Transforms the claimed task into another learning, optimization, or hardness problem whose solution transfers back.

proof_tags.name: Simulation
proof_tags.description: Emulates one oracle, learner, noise process, representation, or computational primitive with another.

proof_tags.name: Symmetrization
proof_tags.description: Introduces ghost samples, random swaps, permutations, or random signs to replace population deviations by sample comparisons.
